name: Telangana Formation Day
description: Celebrates the formation of Telangana as the 29th state of India on June 2, 2014.
promptHint: Telangana state formation celebration, Charminar, Kakatiya dynasty motifs, turquoise and pink colors

name: Bathukamma Festival
description: A floral festival celebrated by women of Telangana, dedicated to Goddess Gauri.
promptHint: Colorful stacked flower arrangement (Bathukamma), women in traditional Telangana sarees, vibrant marigolds and seasonal flowers

name: Bonalu Festival
description: A Hindu festival celebrated in Telangana dedicated to Goddess Mahakali.
promptHint: Women carrying Bonam (decorated pot) on heads, Mahakali temple, red and gold decorations, festive procession

name: Ugadi (Telugu New Year)
description: Telugu New Year celebrated in Telangana and Andhra Pradesh.
promptHint: Ugadi pachadi, mango leaves toran, colorful rangoli, neem flowers, festive Telugu celebration

name: Sammakka Saralamma Jatara
description: Largest tribal festival in Asia, held at Medaram, Telangana. Celebrated biennially in February.
promptHint: Tribal festival in forest, Sammakka goddess idol, red and yellow decorated altars, large gathering in Medaram forest

name: Hyderabad Liberation Day
description: Marks the integration of Hyderabad State into the Indian Union on September 17, 1948.
promptHint: Hyderabad city skyline, Charminar, tricolor flag, historic significance, freedom celebration

name: Charminar Urs
description: Annual festival at Charminar mosque in Hyderabad celebrating the memory of Quli Qutb Shah.
promptHint: Charminar at night with lights, traditional Hyderabadi architecture, lanterns, festive decorations

name: Peerla Panduga
description: Festival observed by Muslims in Telangana with colorful processions and community gatherings.
promptHint: Colorful procession, traditional decorations, crescent and star motifs, Hyderabadi culture

name: Deccan Festival
description: Multi-day cultural festival held in Hyderabad showcasing Deccan art, cuisine, and heritage.
promptHint: Deccan heritage, Biryani, Hyderabadi jewelry, Golconda Fort, classical dance performance, cultural richness

name: Warangal Heritage Week
description: Week-long celebration of the Kakatiya heritage and Warangal Fort, promoting Telangana tourism.
promptHint: Warangal Fort stone pillars, Kakatiya star pattern, ancient Telugu architecture, golden hour lighting

name: Eid ul-Fitr (Hyderabad)
description: Festival marking the end of Ramadan, celebrated with special fervor and grand processions in Hyderabad.
promptHint: Charminar lit up, crescent moon, festive Hyderabadi streets, traditional sherwanis and burqas, lanterns and sweets

name: Sri Rama Navami
description: Celebration of the birth of Lord Rama, widely observed in Telangana temples with processions.
promptHint: Lord Rama with bow and arrow, temple decorated with flowers, golden chariot procession, devotional orange and yellow colors

name: Vinayaka Chavithi
description: Festival celebrating Lord Ganesha's birthday, marked with grand idol installations across Telangana.
promptHint: Beautiful Ganesha clay idol, orange marigold garlands, modak sweets, Hyderabad pandal with lights, festive atmosphere

name: Dasara (Vijaya Dasami)
description: Festival celebrating the victory of good over evil, culminating in Vijayadasami across Telangana.
promptHint: Goddess Durga slaying demon, golden weapons, royal procession, lit diyas, red and gold festival colors

name: Sankranti
description: Harvest festival celebrated across Telangana with kite flying, bonfires, and traditional foods.
promptHint: Colorful kites in blue sky, sugarcane, sesame seeds sweets (til ke laddu), traditional Telugu home with rangoli, cattle decorated with flowers

name: Maha Shivaratri
description: Night-long festival dedicated to Lord Shiva, celebrated with great devotion at Shiva temples across Telangana.
promptHint: Lord Shiva lingam decorated with flowers and bilva leaves, temple lit with oil lamps, blue and silver devotional colors, night sky

name: Telangana Journalists Day
description: Observed to honor the contributions of journalists in shaping the Telangana movement and democratic values.
promptHint: Pen, newspaper, microphone, Telangana state emblem, bold typography, press freedom theme

name: Golconda Fort Light and Sound Show (Annual Inauguration)
description: Annual opening of the famous light and sound show at Golconda Fort, Hyderabad.
promptHint: Golconda Fort dramatically lit at night, colorful light beams, historic Qutb Shahi architecture, dramatic sky

name: Numaish Exhibition
description: Annual industrial and trade exhibition held in Hyderabad, one of the longest-running exhibitions in Asia.
promptHint: Colorful exhibition stalls, Hyderabadi crafts and foods, festive lights, family crowd, vibrant fair atmosphere

name: World Telugu Conference (Vishwa Telugu Mahasabhalu)
description: International gathering of Telugu-speaking people celebrating language, literature, and culture.
promptHint: Telugu script motifs, cultural performances, Telangana and Andhra flags, world map with Telugu diaspora, gold and green colors
